text: Which exact mutually compatible Nixpkgs, Bazel, Clang/LLVM/LLD, and GCC coordinates define M0-REFENV-v1?
why_it_matters: D-003 delegates exact reference-environment values to this domain; the choice fixes lock inputs, toolchain identities, reference predicates, and reproducibility evidence.
unlocks: BUILD-FR-001, BUILD-FR-004, BUILD-FR-005, BUILD-FR-010, and the reference-environment validation matrix.

options.label: Current stable compatible set
options.pros: Current, reversible, and validated in the implementation change that commits immutable locks.
options.cons: Requires the build owner to prove mutual compatibility before Ready.

options.label: Owner enterprise-pinned set
options.pros: Can align with an existing operational standard.
options.cons: No such standard or coordinates are named in the authoritative inputs.

options.label: Oldest packaged set
options.cons: Adds compatibility risk and has no product requirement behind it.

default: Select a current stable mutually compatible set in the first implementation change, commit all immutable locks, and record its validation in a domain ADR before the spec becomes Ready.

answer.text: Option 1: use a current stable mutually compatible Nixpkgs, Bazel, Clang/LLVM/LLD, and GCC set; commit immutable locks and record validation in the M0 reference-environment ADR.
